ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'red wine' class (FOODON_03301636); no specific entry exists for Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GBA0S represents red wine generically. No specific Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC red wine is approximately 150 ml (150 g), equivalent to a single restaurant pour.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174835)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic red table wine used as the closest approved source. Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC is a dry Italian red wine (Montepulciano/Sangiovese blend) with alcohol and macronutrient profile consistent with generic dry red wine. Values adapted from USDA FDC ID 174835. No HPB, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS entry exists for this specific Italian DOC wine.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC specifically. Dry red wine has a GI of approximately 45 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), based on its low fermentable carbohydrate content (~2.6 g/100 ml). GL per 150 g serving = (45 × 2.6 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.76, rounded to 1.8. GI category: low (≤55).

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content and vasodilatory effects; low sodium
hypertension.guidance: Alcohol in red wine can acutely lower blood pressure in small amounts but chronic or heavy consumption raises blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to no more than one standard drink per day and monitor blood pressure response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC is a dry red wine with low residual sugar (~0.62 g/100 ml) and a low GI of approximately 45; however, alcohol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and impair gluconeogenesis, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with food and limit intake to one standard drink per occasion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol content, polyphenols (resveratrol), and absence of saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Moderate red wine consumption has been associated with cardioprotective polyphenol intake (resveratrol, anthocyanins) in observational studies, but current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardiovascular benefit; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should follow their cardiologist's advice on alcohol limits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC is a dry Italian red wine produced in the Marche region of central Italy under the Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) designation. The Superiore classification requires a minimum ageing period and a higher minimum alcohol content than standard Rosso Piceno. The wine is a blend of Montepulciano (at least 60%) and Sangiovese grapes, producing an intense ruby-red colour with purple hues. The aromatic profile features fresh red berries, cherries, and marasca cherry (a sour Marasca cherry variety native to the Adriatic coast), with earthy and spice undertones typical of Montepulciano-dominant blends. On the palate it is medium-to-full bodied with firm tannins, good acidity, and a dry finish. The 2015 vintage from producer Nummaria, estate La Canosa, is served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore. Nutritionally, red wine contains approximately 85 kcal per 100 ml, with negligible protein (0.07 g), no fat, and low residual carbohydrate (~2.6 g/100 ml, predominantly residual sugars). The primary caloric contributor is ethanol (~10–13% ABV). The glycemic index is approximately 45 (low), and the glycemic load per 150 ml serving is approximately 1.8. Clinically, moderate red wine consumption is associated with polyphenol intake but alcohol poses risks for individuals with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. Sulphites are present as a preservative and are a declared allergen.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FDC SR Legacy generic red table wine (FDC ID 174835), which is the closest approved source. No specific nutrient analysis for Rosso Piceno Superiore DOC from Nummaria La Canosa exists in any approved primary database. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for dry red wine. Confidence is medium rather than low because dry red wine is a well-characterised food category with consistent macronutrient profiles across varieties.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174835; wine contains naturally zero fat. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835 for red table wine. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835; wine is a liquid with no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC ID 174835. 2. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect for a wine; corrected to 'Beverages'. 3. This is an alcoholic beverage; is_halal and is_vegan are set to false (wine fining agents such as isinglass or egg white may be used; alcohol renders it non-halal). 4. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no specific DOC wine codes exist in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 5. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this Italian DOC wine; the cuisine is Italian and no mandatory non-English language requirement applies under Veritas v2 multilingual rules (which mandate additional languages only for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes). 6. glycemic_index set to 45 as analogous estimate for dry red wine per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low (45 ≤ 55). 7. All -1 sentinel values have been replaced with actual USDA-reported values where available; no -1 values remain in per_100g.
